question: What method was used for sequencing?

question: Which HIV genes were reported to have been sequenced?
evidence: "Partial pol amplicons (N = 164) and nearly full virus genomes (N = 78) were sequenced. There were 164 HIV-1 strains characterized by partial *pol* sequencing out of a possible 178. Virtually full-length genomes of HIV-1 were amplified from selected strains based on the results of partial pol sequencing."
rationale: The paper mentions sequencing of partial pol amplicons and nearly full virus genomes.

question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from individuals who had previously received ARV drugs?
evidence: To elucidate the virologic origins of the pandemic, a cross-sectional study was conducted of the genetic diversity of HIV-1 strains in villagers in 14 remote locations in Cameroon and in hospitalized and STI patients. In addition, in-patients at two district hospitals (Ndikinimeki, Lomie) and outpatients at STI clinics in those same locations were enrolled in the study. rationale: The paper does not mention obtaining sequences from individuals who had previously received ARV drugs. The study focuses on genetic diversity in rural populations and hospitalized patients, but does not specify treatment history.
